reps.improvement_area_short: E (Emotional Labelling) — avg 2.2/5, bottom dimension across 4/5 calls. Consistently uses generic labels ('it sounds tough', 'a lot going on') instead of naming the parent's specific emotion using their exact words. P3 deepening (I=3.0) is borderline — emotional labelling failure is THE limiting factor.

BUYING SIGNAL RECOGNITION + PRICE CARD are now #1 priority. Jo Kelly PREDICTION MISMATCH confirms: deepening CAN reach 6.8 quality but without cycling the abandonment bomb, the emotional anchor doesn't hold. Two things must happen: (1) Memorise price card — $2,700 lump / $450 fortnightly / $225 weekly / $900 monthly. No on-call calculation. Ever. (2) When mum reveals something heavy (Tyson's abandonment, Bianca's knife incident) — STOP and ask 2 more questions before moving. Not 1. Two.
N/A — exited DC role. Reference only.

CRITICAL: I Score = 1.0, E Score = 1.0 — no emotional deepening present in any scored call. All three calls show minimal engagement with parent pain. Transcripts unavailable — scoring confidence low, but direction clear. FALSE POSITIVE on Jasmine Lund is now a documented teaching case: close without emotional anchoring = reversal within 7 days. Discovery/deepening fundamentals required before any close skill development.

P3 Deepening (I=3) — single gap. When Bec said 'my biggest concern is that it kills him' Tom replied 'Yeah yeah yeah. But I think what's hard is there's so many problems at the moment...' — pivoted. Gold standard: pause, repeat her words, ask 'Tell me more about that.' This is the ONLY gap between Tom's current 7.5 and an 8.5+ score. Everything else is already above rep average.

top_objections.note: Most common AND worst-handled category. Five instances, avg grade 1.8. Rye's Bianca JESS (grade 1) is the worst on record. The contrast: Tom's Bec Richardson — price stated confidently ($2,700), once, no calculation. All reps must memorise the price card. 'I'll find a way' is now a documented buying signal — it is a YES, not a stall.

All three from Anthony. Range of 2-4/5 shows the technique exists but isn't systematic. Gets it right with isolation ('if dad wasn't on board, what would happen?') but defaults to BAMFAM when he doesn't think to isolate. The $111 de-risk frame was not used in any partner objection this week.

Best-handled category. Tom's dentist metaphor on Bec Richardson (grade 5) is gold standard — pre-emptive, natural, from a confirmed close. This is what objection handling from depth looks like. The objection was absorbed before it became a blocker.
